opcode.name: OP_PlayerProfile
opcode.comment: EQ Legends char profile (~38KB): hdr race@21/class@25/level@33; bind@39; name deep. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_ZoneEntry
opcode.comment: ZoneEntryCode

opcode.name: OP_TimeOfDay
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation; 8B stock timeOfDayStruct byte-identical (wire-verified 8B); advances at EQ game rate

opcode.name: OP_NewZone
opcode.comment: EQ Legends current zone (S>C, ~340B, once per zone-in). Packed null-terminated short_name + long_name (+ zonefile repeat + binary tail). Confirmed 3-way: guktop/'The City of Guk', nektulos/'Nektulos Forest', unrest/'The Estate of Unrest' — each the correct current zone, each a different length. Decoded by EqlDispatch::newZone -> ZoneMgr::setZoneByName. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_ZoneBindMarker
opcode.comment: EQ Legends bind-point marker (14B S>C, once at zone-in). Carries the BIND zone id (u32@6), identical across zones (nektulos-vs-upperguk both read 25) — NOT the current zone. Was mis-labeled OP_NewZone until 2026-07-08. Unwired. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_SpawnDoor
opcode.comment: DoorSpawnsCode

opcode.name: OP_GroundSpawn
opcode.comment: MakeDropCode

opcode.name: OP_SendZonePoints
opcode.comment: Coords in a zone that will port you to another zone

opcode.name: OP_AAExpUpdate
opcode.comment: Receiving AA experience. Also when percent to AA changes.

opcode.name: OP_ExpUpdate
opcode.comment: ExpUpdateCode

opcode.name: OP_GuildMOTD
opcode.comment: GuildMOTD

opcode.name: OP_ClientUpdate
opcode.comment: EQ Legends: C>S self-position (42B, wired). A 28B S>C variant carries OTHER-PLAYER positions (own byte-aligned pos+vel layout, not MobUpdate's packing) — identified 2026-07-08, field layout partial (needs a 2nd-char walk capture to finalize), still unwired. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_ZoneSpawns
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spawn info (S>C): null-term name + variable block; position = three u32 words at the block tail (Z/Y/X @ len-95/-91/-87), each a signed 19-bit fixed-point (x8) coord in the word's low bits — same packing as Live spawnStruct position words. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_NpcMoveUpdate
opcode.comment: EQ Legends per-NPC movement stream (S>C, variable 15-21B). Byte-identical bit format to Live OP_NpcMoveUpdate (16b BE spawnId + 16b pad + 6b fieldmask + 19/19/19 y/x/z sign-magnitude >>3 + 12b heading + optional deltas); decoded by the shared decode_npc_move_update. Confirmed 2026-07-08: all 51 ids + positions match the 0x67e0 OP_MobUpdate stream. Prior 917c was a dead mapping (never seen on the wire). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_MobUpdate
opcode.comment: EQ Legends per-mob position update (S>C, 14B). Byte-identical to Live spawnPositionUpdate (packed y:19/z:19/u3:7/x:19/heading:12 fixed-point x8, spawnId u16@0 + 2 zero bytes); decoded by the shared decode_mob_update. The earlier i16-offset read (Y@4/8, Z@6/64, X@10) was a truncated window of these bitfields — the source of the 8192-unit north<->south Y wrap. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_DeleteSpawn
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spawn removal / death (S>C, 4B {u32 spawnId}, deleteSpawnStruct). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08 by time-correlation: 8/9 ids stop receiving OP_MobUpdate position updates at/after this event (mobs killed over a combat session). Lights up the already-wired SpawnShell::deleteSpawn. NOTE: 0x67a8 is the LOOK-ALIKE 4B {id} op but is combat engage/disengage (ids keep moving after; id=0 clears) — NOT despawn. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_RemoveSpawn
opcode.comment: Remove spawn from zone

opcode.name: OP_Death
opcode.comment: EQ Legends death/corpse (S>C, 40B = newCorpseStruct, n=8). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: decodes field-for-field — victim u32@0 (all 8 ∈ the OP_DeleteSpawn 0x59a1 set), killer u32@4 (=player 13167 every kill), corpse type i32@12, killing-blow spellId u32@16 (-1=melee), damage u32@24 (9-80). Lights up the already-wired SpawnShell::killSpawn → SpawnKilled. Fires just before the matching OP_DeleteSpawn. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_WearChange
opcode.comment: Spawn-state / appearance broadcast (multi-slot). Header=wearChangeStruct(11B), then slotCount*wearChangeSlotStruct(16B+varname), then 2B pad. count=0→13B (hash refresh), count=N→header+N slots (~+23B/slot incl short name). slotCount@9 correlates with size; unk8=1 in equipment samples. Re-confirmed equipment form 2026-05-27 (wearchange-clean: 30 fires on equip/remove, 13B refresh, field@4=material code). POLYMORPHIC: this same wire opcode also carries buff-icon arrays (21B blocks: {spellID, value, caster-name\0}) as a spawn-state broadcast — multiple spawn ids, fires on zone-in and around buff events. Those variants don't match SpawnUpdateStruct (SZC_Match) so they're unhandled, but appear in --list-events/--dump-payload — do NOT mistake them for a buff/BuffQuery opcode (cost a full 2026-05-27 hunt). Dedicated buff broadcast is OP_Buff 0x18b4 (trailing flag distinguishes).

opcode.name: OP_SpawnAppearance
opcode.comment: SpawnAppearanceCode

opcode.name: OP_SpawnAppearance2
opcode.comment: EQ Legends extended appearance (24B) — byte-identical to Live spawnAppearance2Struct {u32 spawnId, u32 type, u32 value, u8[12] pad}. Confirmed 2026-07-08: 55 spawn ids, type set {5,6,8,22,26,...} matches Live's {5,6,8,22,26,36,42}; type->value fits (type 6->100/110/115, type 43->0/1/2 flag). Wired S>C to the neutral SpawnShell::updateSpawnLock (acts on type 0x2c mob-lock; no-op on other types). C>S half (ClickBuffOff, type 0x24) acknowledged, unwired. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Stamina
opcode.comment: Server updating on hunger/thirst

opcode.name: OP_HPUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation; multiplexed stat channel (subtype byte @4). 6B subtype-0x02 = HP-bar feed (u16 id, u16 0, 0x02, u8 hp%); decoded by EqlDispatch::hpUpdate. Other sizes/subtypes are cur/max i64 stat pairs (dropped)

opcode.name: OP_GuildMemberUpdate
opcode.comment: Info regarding guild members

opcode.name: OP_ClickObject
opcode.comment: Items dropped on the ground

opcode.name: OP_Action
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spell/special action (S>C). Live paired send: 64B actionStruct (n=97/117) + 88B actionAltStruct (n=22/43); target/source u16@0/@2, spell u16@4 (real ids 502/445/821). Sole 64B/88B op. Lights up the already-wired SpellShell::action (both payloads). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Action2
opcode.comment: EQ Legends melee combat / damage-resolution stream (S>C, 48B fixed, n=481/968). Byte-identical to Live action2Struct: target u16@0, source u16@2, damage i32@8, spell i32@20 (-1=melee), type u8@40; @24-39 = knockback floats. Sole 48B S>C op; cross-val upperguk+fulllogin. Lights up the already-wired CombatRouter::action2. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Consider
opcode.comment: EQ Legends /consider (post-2026-07-07 re-map). 24B {u32 self, u32 target, u32 faction, u32 =7}: C>S request has faction=0, S>C reply fills faction (2=warmly, 4=amiably; level comes from the spawn, not here). Decoded by seq-backend-eql parse_legends_consider into the shared Consider, then the neutral SpawnShell::consMessage. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_TargetMouse
opcode.comment: EQ Legends C>S target select. Payload is byte-identical to Live's clientTargetStruct ({u32 spawn_id}, 0 = clear/untarget), so it wires straight to the neutral SpawnShell::clientTarget — no Legends-specific decode. Confirmed 2026-07-07 across fight + Nektulos locclip captures (every payload resolves to a 0x7475 spawn id or 0). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_SpawnRename
opcode.comment: Spawns getting renamed after initial NewSpawn

opcode.name: OP_Illusion
opcode.comment: Spawn being illusioned (changing forms)

opcode.name: OP_Shroud
opcode.comment: Server putting players into shroud form

opcode.name: OP_ZoneChange
opcode.comment: old ZoneChangeCode

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite
opcode.comment: You invite someone while ungrouped or get invited by someone ungrouped

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite2
opcode.comment: Invite into an EXISTING group (inviter already grouped); OP_GroupInvite (ee64) is used to form a NEW group. Same groupInviteStruct layout (invitee@0, inviter@64), C>S 168B. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-invite2: fired only when adding a 3rd member, naming the same inviter + new invitee).

opcode.name: OP_GroupCancelInvite
opcode.comment: Client-side decline-invite OR leave/disband-group request (168 bytes; name fields = self+self when leaving). C>S only in observed captures. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-2variants: only C>S group-action unknown, fires before disband, self+self).

opcode.name: OP_GroupFollow
opcode.comment: Member added (modern: 68 bytes, name at offset 0).

opcode.name: OP_GroupFollow2
opcode.comment: Player joins your group (168 bytes; member name at offset 64, leading 64 bytes zero, lean tail w/ level field). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-2variants: fires per form, S>C, simplest of the two form-time structs).

opcode.name: OP_GroupUpdate
opcode.comment: Per-slot group status (modern: 92 bytes, recipient name + slot). Handler currently noops; member identity comes from OP_GroupFollow.

opcode.name: OP_GroupDisband
opcode.comment: Your group dissolved (modern: 168 bytes; membername=self at offset 64)

opcode.name: OP_GroupDisband2
opcode.comment: S>C to all STAYERS when any member departs (not leader-only). 168 bytes; membername=peer at offset 64.

opcode.name: OP_GroupInviteAccepted
opcode.comment: S>C to inviter when invitee accepts ungrouped invite. Fires in same bundle as OP_GroupMemberList + OP_GroupFollow2 (join-complete). 168 bytes; invitee name at offset 64.

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite2Sent
opcode.comment: S>C to leader immediately when grouped invite (OP_GroupInvite2) is sent — fires before invitee accepts. Leader-only. 168 bytes; both names filled (leader at 0, invitee at 64).

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberLeave
opcode.comment: S>C broadcast to remaining members when any member leaves. 168 bytes. Fires only when ≥2 members remain after departure; name at offset 64 encodes updated group state.

opcode.name: OP_GroupLeader
opcode.comment: Group leader change (modern: 80 bytes)

opcode.name: OP_Buff
opcode.comment: S>C buff broadcast — variable length, no legacy 168b buffStruct fit. Observed forms: 13b (likely fade: {u32 spawnID, u32 spellID, u16=0x0001, u8=0, u8 slot, u8=0}); 30b (single self-buff add, no caster); 34/51/53/59b (one or two embedded blocks each carrying {tag=0x036a u32, counter u32, padding, caster-name\0}); 85b (3-block form). Pre-patch OP_Buff 0x55b9 no longer fires; this slot carries all buff broadcasts. Confirmed across 53 fires (3 captures) — only opcode firing this variable-size buff-shaped pattern.

opcode.name: OP_BeginCast
opcode.comment: BeginCastCode - S>C broadcast: {u32 spellId, u16 casterSpawnId, u16 castTime_ms, u8[7] pad}. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (castTime decodes to real ms: 4000/6500/9000).

opcode.name: OP_CastSpell
opcode.comment: StartCastCode - C>S 39B: {i32 gemSlot, u32 spellId, u8[10] 0xff, u32 targetId, ...}. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch.

opcode.name: OP_SwapSpell
opcode.comment: TradeSpellBookSlotsCode

opcode.name: OP_MemorizeSpell
opcode.comment: Bidirectional 16B: {u32 gem_slot, u32 spellId, int16 action, int16 pad, u8[4] unknown}. action=1 C>S begin memorizing (no ack); action=2 C>S+S>C memorize complete (client shows 'You have finished memorizing'); action=3 C>S+S>C zone-in spell sync (declare existing spell in slot); action=4 observed post-patch (gem clear/forget, semantics TBD). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch: 11 C>S + 11 S>C balanced.

opcode.name: OP_InspectAnswer
opcode.comment: InspectDataCode

opcode.name: OP_Emote
opcode.comment: EmoteTextCode

opcode.name: OP_SimpleMessage
opcode.comment: SimpleMessageCode

opcode.name: OP_FormattedMessage
opcode.comment: FormattedMessageCode i.e. pet dismissed etc

opcode.name: OP_CommonMessage
opcode.comment: ChannelMessageCode i.e. /tell /ooc /shout etc

opcode.name: OP_SpecialMesg
opcode.comment: Communicate textual info to client including hail responses etc

opcode.name: OP_RandomReq
opcode.comment: RandomReqCode - C>S 8 bytes: {u32 bottom, u32 top}

opcode.name: OP_RandomReply
opcode.comment: RandomCode - S>C 76 bytes broadcast: {u32 bottom, u32 top, u32 result, char name[64]}

opcode.name: OP_ConsentResponse
opcode.comment: Server replying with consent information after /consent

opcode.name: OP_DenyResponse
opcode.comment: Server replying with deny information after /deny

opcode.name: OP_ManaChange
opcode.comment: Resource update after spell/ability use (S>C, 20b): {i32 curMana, i32 curEndurance, i32 spellOrAbilityId, u8[8]}. Fires once per cast or combat ability; spellId@8 cross-refs OP_CastSpell. field4 = current endurance, NOT max mana (legacy label wrong) — confirmed 2026-05-26 by cross-ref against OP_EndUpdate cur. EQ Legends id=0x07c9 confirmed 2026-07-08 by value-match (curMana 282-930, endurance ~800, spellId 445/821 cross-ref OP_Action) — byte-identical to manaDecrementStruct; lights up the already-wired Player::manaChange. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_BazaarSearchRequest
opcode.comment: Bazaar search request

opcode.name: OP_BazaarSearchResponse
opcode.comment: Bazaar search Response - Struct incorrect

opcode.name: OP_MoneyOnCorpse
opcode.comment: MoneyOnCorpseCode

opcode.name: OP_SkillUpdate
opcode.comment: Skill up code

opcode.name: OP_LevelUpdate
opcode.comment: LevelUpUpdateCode

opcode.name: OP_DzSwitchInfo
opcode.comment: Expedition compass etc

opcode.name: OP_DzInfo
opcode.comment: Expedition Information

opcode.name: OP_EnvDamage
opcode.comment: Environmental Damage

opcode.name: OP_SetRunMode
opcode.comment: old cRunToggleCode

opcode.name: OP_Trader
opcode.comment: PC's turning trader on and off

opcode.name: OP_ItemLinkResponse
opcode.comment: ItemInfoCode

opcode.name: OP_BuffFadeMsg
opcode.comment: SpellFadeCode

opcode.name: OP_CorpseLocResponse
opcode.comment: old CorpseLocCode:

opcode.name: OP_ItemPacket
opcode.comment: EQ Legends bulk item transfer (S>C, ~1.1KB-19KB, sent on zone-in). u32@4 = item count N, then N item records ~1122-1130b each (16-char serial + name×2 + Trophy/Benefit descriptor + stats; item-id candidate = first u32). IDENTIFIED, NOT DECODED — the record layout (3-class fields etc.) is future work, and the Legends format differs from Live's itemPacketStruct, so this is intentionally NOT wired to the itemCache sink. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_LeaderExpUpdate
opcode.comment: Leadership AA Exp Update

opcode.name: OP_MovementHistory
opcode.comment: Movement history for speed/movement hack detection

opcode.name: OP_UIUpdate
opcode.comment: Seems to be sent to handle a variety of UI updates - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberInfo
opcode.comment: Recipient-only group event notification (modern: 168 bytes; recipient name@64, leading 64b zero, tail u32 epoch + group_id + spawn_id fields). NOT the actual roster — that's OP_GroupMemberList=0x312a. Misidentified previously based on 'fires per form-event' shape alone; content fingerprint shows only the recipient's own name with no peer identity. Kept named distinctly to avoid confusion; needs a real purpose attribution.

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberList
opcode.comment: Modern group roster broadcast. Variable length (54-301b observed: 1-2 members at 54-107b, 6-member group at 301b). Wire layout: {u32 groupId, u32 memberCount} header, then a slot-0 leader extended record (2 null-padded name fields + u32 level/class + flags), then (memberCount-1) regular slot records each prefixed with u32 slotIdx (1, 2, …) and a null-padded name. Fires S>C at zone-in (existing roster recovery) AND on every roster mutation. Confirmed against groupbuff-repro.vpk (6-member, leader name + 5 slot markers found at 48-byte intervals starting at offset 0x42) and group-2variants.vpk (1- and 2-member cases).

opcode.name: OP_GuildMemberList
opcode.comment: List of guild members - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_ManaUpdate
opcode.comment: Mana bar update - 10 bytes (spawn_id u16, cur u32, max u32). Per-spawn broadcast (not just local player); local player shows +10/tick regen. Same wire shape as OP_EndUpdate. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch.

opcode.name: OP_EndUpdate
opcode.comment: Endurance Update opcode - 10 bytes (spawn_id u16, cur u32, max u32)

opcode.name: OP_Marketplace
opcode.comment: Marketplace data - Guessing variable length 11444 bytes as of 11/28/12

opcode.name: OP_MarketplaceSelect
opcode.comment: Sent when highlighting an item in Marketplace - 80 bytes

opcode.name: OP_DzMembers
opcode.comment: Expedition Members - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Campfire
opcode.comment: Fellowship campfire information - 1076 bytes

opcode.name: OP_SelectCampfire
opcode.comment: Fellowship campfire Choices - Guessing variable length

opcode.name: OP_Claims
opcode.comment: Contents of claims window. /claim then refresh to capture packet - Guessing variable length

opcode.name: OP_VoiceChat
opcode.comment: Voice chat server info - Variable length (Data sent when joining group,raid etc)

opcode.name: OP_PollQuestions
opcode.comment: SOE in-game player poll questions - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_PollResponses
opcode.comment: Poll response choices - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_ShroudProgression
opcode.comment: Unlocked shrouds - 293 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ShroudTemplates
opcode.comment: Shroud templates to choose from on shroud NPC - 18983 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Fellowship
opcode.comment: Fellowship information - 2576 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ExpandedGuildInfo
opcode.comment: Guild ranks and other misc guild data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_GuildBank
opcode.comment: Guild bank contents - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_OpenTradeskillContainer
opcode.comment: Opens forge, kiln etc - 92 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeskillRecipes
opcode.comment: Learned recipes - 84 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillCombine
opcode.comment: Tradeskill combine using new tradeskill window - 40 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillResult
opcode.comment: Packets sent when tradeskill combine successful equals # of ingredients used in combine?- 12 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillCombineOld
opcode.comment: Tradeskill combine using old tradeskill window - 24 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_ItemPlayerPacket
opcode.comment: Inventory/bank items coming over during zone - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_TaskDescription
opcode.comment: Task descriptions coming down for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_TaskActivity
opcode.comment: Task activity descriptions coming down for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_CompletedTasks
opcode.comment: Task history for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_CustomTitles
opcode.comment: List of available titles - 1520 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Animation
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spawn animation broadcast (S>C, 4B, n=354/718). Byte-identical to Live animationStruct: spawnId u16@0, action u8@2 (1-46), speed u8@3 (=10 across all 354). No handler wired (animation not surfaced) — id remapped so it resolves cleanly in opcode-stats. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_WhoAllRequest
opcode.comment: WhoAllReqCode - 156 bytes

opcode.name: OP_WhoAllResponse
opcode.comment: WhoAllOutputCode - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_InspectRequest
opcode.comment: InspectRequestCode - 8 bytes C>S: {u32 target_spawn_id, u32 self_spawn_id}

opcode.name: OP_GetGuildMOTD
opcode.comment: Request guildMOTD - 648 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeRequest
opcode.comment: You request trade session - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeDeny
opcode.comment: Target not ready to trade - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeRequestAck
opcode.comment: Trade request recipient is acknowledging they are able to trade - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_CancelTrade
opcode.comment: Cancel a trade window - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_FinishTrade
opcode.comment: Trade is over - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeCoins
opcode.comment: You put coins put in a trade window - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeCoins2
opcode.comment: Someone else puts coins put in your trade window - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeAcceptClick
opcode.comment: One side clicks Accept on the trade 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ItemLinkClick
opcode.comment: Click on itemlinks - 52 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LootRequest
opcode.comment: LootCorpseCode - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LootItem
opcode.comment: Loot item from corpse - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_EndLootRequest
opcode.comment: DoneLootingCode - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Track
opcode.comment: Tracking data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_AutoAttack
opcode.comment: Attack on/off - C>S 4 bytes: {u32 mode} (1=on, 0=off). Fires first of the paired AutoAttack+AutoAttack2 sends.

opcode.name: OP_AutoAttack2
opcode.comment: Attack on/off - C>S 4 bytes: {u32 mode} (1=on, 0=off). Fires second, paired with OP_AutoAttack on every toggle.

opcode.name: OP_ClickDoor
opcode.comment: DoorOpenCode click request from client - 16 bytes

opcode.name: OP_MoveDoor
opcode.comment: DoorClickCode response from server - 2 bytes

opcode.name: OP_MoveItem
opcode.comment: Client moving an item from one slot to another - 28 bytes. Resolved via inventory.opcodestats.txt: 30 C>S fires at exactly 28 bytes during a slot-swap session, no competing unknown opcode at that size+direction. Wire layout: two 12-byte invSlotStruct descriptors + uint32 stack count.

opcode.name: OP_Jump
opcode.comment: JumpCode - 0 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TGB
opcode.comment: Client telling server to set targetgroupbuff - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Lockouts
opcode.comment: Expedition lockouts

opcode.name: OP_RaidInvite
opcode.comment: Client side raid invite requests 140 bytes

opcode.name: OP_RaidJoin
opcode.comment: Server side raid information - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Feedback
opcode.comment: Client sending feedback to server - 1148 bytes

opcode.name: OP_GuildsList
opcode.comment: Listing of all guilds. Can be triggered by /lfg search?

opcode.name: OP_GuildsInZoneList
opcode.comment: Listing of guild names present in the current zone. Generally seen on zoning

opcode.name: OP_NewGuildInZone
opcode.comment: An additional guild name has appeared in the current zone. Seen when a guilded player enters the current zone

opcode.name: OP_LFGGetMatchesRequest
opcode.comment: LFG/LFP client request - 16 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LFGGetMatchesResponse
opcode.comment: LFG/LFP server response - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_MercenaryList
opcode.comment: Listing of hired mercenaries - 648 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ClickInventory
opcode.comment: Click items from inventory or armor - 16 bytes 5th byte is Slot ID

opcode.name: OP_ClickInventoryAck
opcode.comment: Server acknowledges click from inventory or armor - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_BuffQuery
opcode.comment: C>S: {u32 buff_slot, u32 self_spawn_id} (8 bytes). Client requests server reconfirm buff slot N at zone-in. Fires once per occupied buff slot on the SECOND zone-in after a buff was applied (not the first — the buff must survive one full zone cycle before the client requests verification). Server responds with OP_Buff for that slot. Wire-verified: slot=0 → OP_Buff IVU(225) spellslot=0; slot=1 → DE(352) spellslot=1; slot=2/1/0 → SLW/SoW/Regen.

opcode.name: OP_BuffWindow
opcode.comment: EQ Legends buff-window init (S>C, 12B = buffWindowSlotStruct, fires 10x/zone-in for slots 0-9). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: 10 fires, slot u32@0 = 0..9, spellid u32@4 = 0xffffffff (empty), pad u32@8 = 0 — byte-identical to Live. NOTE: no OP_BuffWindow handler is wired on eql yet (id-only; resolves in opcode-stats but not surfaced). Layout: {u32 slot, u32 spellid=0xffffffff_empty, u32 pad}; long-duration buffs come via OP_Buff / the buff-bar sync; never fires mid-session.

opcode.name: OP_SpawnAppearance2
opcode.comment: Extended 24-byte bidirectional appearance update. Payload: {u32 spawn_id, u32 type, u32 value, u8[12] pad}. C>S type=0x24 (36) with spawn_id=0 = OP_ClickBuffOff (buff_slot in value field). S>C fires carry guild/cast/appearance types and co-occur with buff landing messages. Distinct from OP_SpawnAppearance. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch: type set {5,6,8,22,26,36,42} matches; ClickBuffOff C>S type=0x24 fires with spawn_id=0, varying buff_slot. type=0x2c (44) S>C = TLP-ruleset mob-lock / FTE attackable flag: value=0 = attackable (fires ~5-7s after a fresh spawn's OP_ZoneEntry — the grey→attackable transition), value=1 = locked/engaged (fires when a mob is claimed; observed firing for the engaged target exactly at engage time). Per-spawn 0/1 toggle, balanced across a session; absent on standard Live (mobs always attackable). Confirmed 2026-05-27 on a locked TLP server (moblock.vpk): fresh spawns 14245/14247/14264 each spawn→value=0 after ~5-7s; engaged target 13799 → value=1 at engage. Wired to Spawn.locked → web UI.

opcode.name: OP_HouseAddress
opcode.comment: House and guildhall address information - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_HouseContents
opcode.comment: Server sending house information and contents - variable length

opcode.name: OP_FriendsList
opcode.comment: Server sending friends list contents - variable length (Sent when choosing "Welcome" from EQ button)

opcode.name: OP_FriendsOnline
opcode.comment: Friends currently online - variable length (Sent when choosing "Welcome" form EQ button)

opcode.name: OP_Rewards
opcode.comment: Server sending pending rewards - variable length

opcode.name: OP_FTPNags
opcode.comment: Free to play nags and other data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Find
opcode.comment: Find window data - variable length, 24 min

opcode.name: OP_ConsiderCorpse
opcode.comment: ConCorpseCode - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TributeUpdate
opcode.comment: Tribute and trophy skills - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Unknown1
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_Unknown2
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_Unknown3
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_RequestZoneChange
opcode.comment: Send by server when a click causes zone. Also, origin

opcode.name: OP_SendAATable
opcode.comment: Static AA ability-definition menu sent S>C on zone-in. Variable-length: u32 count + count records of base 44B + per-record prereq trailer.

opcode.name: OP_AckPacket
opcode.comment: Appears to be generic ack at the presentation level

opcode.name: OP_Weather
opcode.comment: old WeatherCode

opcode.name: OP_ReqNewZone
opcode.comment: Client requesting NewZone_Struct

opcode.name: OP_ReqClientSpawn
opcode.comment: Client requesting spawn data

opcode.name: OP_SendExpZonein
opcode.comment: Both directions. Negotiating sending of exp info.

opcode.name: OP_AAAction
opcode.comment: Dual-form: C>S 16b aaActionStruct {action, ability, unknown, exp_value} for buy/activate (action=3=buy), plus S>C 12b companion server messages (fire frequently). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (aaaction-spend): the single C>S 16b buy fire decoded action=3 ability=224 (Mental Clarity rank purchase) alongside ~100 S>C 12b companions.

opcode.name: OP_SetServerFilter
opcode.comment: Client telling server /filter information

opcode.name: OP_GuildTributeStatus
opcode.comment: Guild tribute stats send on zone

opcode.name: OP_GuildTributeInfo
opcode.comment: Guild tribute loadout, only if on

opcode.name: OP_RespawnWindow
opcode.comment: Server telling client enough to populate the respawn window when you die

opcode.name: OP_InitialMobHealth
opcode.comment: Initial health sent when a player clicks on the mob. Subsequent updated will be OP_MobHealth

opcode.name: OP_MobHealth
opcode.comment: health sent when a player clicks on the mob

opcode.name: OP_DeltaCheck
opcode.comment: Client sending server delta information.

opcode.name: OP_LoadSpellSet
opcode.comment: /mem spellsetname

opcode.name: OP_Consume
opcode.comment: Client force feeding food/drink

opcode.name: OP_LFGCommand
opcode.comment: old LFGReqCode

opcode.name: OP_Bug
opcode.comment: /bug

opcode.name: OP_Save
opcode.comment: Client asking server to save user state

opcode.name: OP_Camp
opcode.comment: old cStartCampingCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopPlayerSell
opcode.comment: old SellItemCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopEnd
opcode.comment: old CloseVendorCode

opcode.name: OP_SenseHeading
opcode.comment: old cSenseHeadingCode

opcode.name: OP_LootComplete
opcode.comment: old sDoneLootingCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopRequest
opcode.comment: old OpenVendorCode

opcode.name: OP_FaceChange
opcode.comment: /face

opcode.name: OP_Sneak
opcode.comment: Clicked sneak

opcode.name: OP_DisarmTraps
opcode.comment: Clicked disarm traps

opcode.name: OP_Forage
opcode.comment: old ForageCode

opcode.name: OP_GMKill
opcode.comment: GM /kill - Insta kill mob/pc

opcode.name: OP_ShopPlayerBuy
opcode.comment: old BuyItemCode

opcode.name: OP_LFPCommand
opcode.comment: looking for players

opcode.name: OP_ConfirmDelete
opcode.comment: Client sends this to server to confirm op_deletespawn

opcode.name: OP_TargetCommand
opcode.comment: Target user

opcode.name: OP_FindPersonRequest
opcode.comment: Control-F Find

opcode.name: OP_FindResponse
opcode.comment: Response to control-F

opcode.name: OP_GMLastName
opcode.comment: GM /lastname - Change user lastname

opcode.name: OP_TributeInfo
opcode.comment: Tribute information

opcode.name: OP_RespondAA
opcode.comment: Server response to OP_AAAction; carries 12B records (ability_id, rank, _) for player's full AA list incl. auto-grants and purchased ranks
